title: Public U-Boot A733 MMC/SPL handoff audit
question: Does the local public A733 U-Boot/SPL reference reveal a source-backed SDMMC0 handoff, fabric, firewall, or IDMAC setup bit missing from Linux?

sources.subject: config: a733: add initial support for Cubie A7A

findings: The A733 U-Boot reference tree exists locally on Strix and is a public branch from https://github.com/dlan17/u-boot.git.
drivers/mmc/sunxi_mmc.c adds A733 clock math: PERIPH-derived MMC parent rate divided by 3, second-divider encoding, and A733 module-clock register offset 0xd00 with 0x10 stride.
The U-Boot sunxi MMC transfer path uses CPU/FIFO PIO and sets SUNXI_MMC_GCTRL_ACCESS_BY_AHB before data transfer; it does not exercise normal descriptor IDMAC fetch.
The visible A733 DT and U-Boot code do not name an SDMMC0 NSI/IOMMU/firewall/security master handoff bit.
A733 U-Boot board DTS contains vendor-style allwinner,pinmux properties and extra peripherals, but these are not upstream Linux DTS inputs and do not explain IDMAC descriptor memory access.
decision: No kernel patch. Public U-Boot corroborates clock/register placement and PIO viability, but provides no source-backed Linux normal-IDMAC descriptor-fetch fix.
next_responsible_step: Keep waiting for maintainer/list/vendor reply or continue only with source-backed SDMMC0 master/firewall/security evidence; do not promote speculative DTS or Ethernet/display/USB work.
